In a small town in the Hautes-Pyrénées, Emmanuel Macron tries to suspend time                Faced with geopolitical emergencies and internal tensions within France, the French president has chosen to enter what he calls a 'decanting phase' before the appointment of his new government.

Pelosi visits Kyiv as residents are evacuated from Mariupol steel plant  Partial evacuations of the Azovstal steelworks have been carried out but thousands of people remain trapped in the southeastern city.

Subscribers onlyPolluting chemicals detected in eight high altitude lakes  in the French Pyrénées  Among the 151 chemical molecules detected, researchers found pesticides, fungicides, commonly used biocides and 60 miscellaneous compounds used in perfumes and cleaning products.

Subscribers onlyThe European Commission is targeting gag procedures used to intimidate journalists and whistleblowers  A draft European directive intends to limit the rise of a legal practice designed to intimidate people.

Feature   Flora Genoux Subscribers onlyIn Chile, the Mapuche Indians want to reclaim their land The historical disagreement over the restitution of the ancestral lands of the Mapuche indigenous population, which has been plagued by violence and illegal occupations, has reached a turning point as a leftist president, Gabriel Boric, is holding the reins.   8 min read

Investigation   Joséphine Lebard Subscribers onlyDriven by Instagram, tattoos are becoming more popular and more political Tattooing is booming among young people, especially thanks to social networks. Beyond the aesthetic appeal, some see it as a way to claim an identity or to 'make peace' with their body and their personal history.   6 min read

Feature   Camille Labro 'No one understood that a good savory breakfast could be a thing of the future' Inspired by their travels in Vancouver and Melbourne, in 2013 Sarah Mouchot and Nicolas Alary opened the restaurant Holybelly in Paris. From the morning until 5:00 p.m., they serve consistent, high-quality cuisine in their two restaurants on the same street.   2 min read

Feature   Emmanuel Grynszpan and  Laurence Geai Two weeks of war on the Donbas front Photographer Laurence Geai, special correspondent for 'Le Monde' in Ukraine with reporter Emmanuel Grynszpan, reports on daily life during the war in the Donetsk and Luhansk regions, where Russian forces are focusing most of the fighting.   2 min read
